description: Primary-sourced accessibility rules — WCAG 2.2 success criteria + ARIA APG component contracts. Threshold-bearing + cited (re-derived from w3.org, not the reference skill's secondhand CSV).

rules.title: Text contrast
rules.rule: Body text contrast ratio >= 4.5:1; large text (>= 18pt, or >= 14pt bold) >= 3:1. Computed with no rounding.
rules.dont: Gray-on-gray (#9CA3AF on #F3F4F6 ~ 3.2:1) fails AA for body text.

rules.title: Non-text contrast
rules.rule: UI components (borders of inputs, focus indicators, toggle states) and meaningful graphics >= 3:1 against adjacent colors.
rules.dont: A 1px #E5E7EB input border on white (~1.2:1) is invisible to low-vision users.

rules.title: Target size (minimum)
rules.rule: Interactive targets >= 24x24 CSS px (or have >= 24px spacing), unless an equivalent control exists. iOS HIG recommends 44pt; Material 48dp.
rules.do: Hit area >= 24px even if the icon is smaller (pad it).

rules.title: Focus visible
rules.rule: Every keyboard-focusable element shows a visible focus indicator. Never `outline: none` without a replacement.
rules.do: Use :focus-visible with a >= 2px ring meeting 3:1 contrast (SC 1.4.11).

rules.title: Focus not obscured (minimum)
rules.rule: A focused element must not be entirely hidden by sticky headers/footers or other author content.
rules.dont: A sticky header that covers the focused input when tabbing.

rules.title: Dragging movements
rules.rule: Any drag operation has a single-pointer (tap/click) alternative, unless dragging is essential.
rules.do: A reorderable list also offers up/down buttons.

rules.title: Non-text content (alt text)
rules.rule: Every img/area/input[type=image] has alt; decorative images use alt="" (or role=presentation) so AT skips them.

rules.title: Keyboard operable
rules.rule: All functionality is operable by keyboard alone (no mouse-only handlers). Custom widgets implement the expected key interactions.
rules.dont: A <div onClick> with no role/tabindex/keydown is keyboard-dead.

rules.title: First rule of ARIA — prefer native HTML
rules.rule: Use a native element with the semantics/behavior you need before adding ARIA. Only reach for ARIA to fill a genuine gap.
rules.bad: <div role="button" tabindex="0"> re-implementing all of the above

source.name: ARIA APG
source.ref: No ARIA is better than bad ARIA

rules.title: Modal dialog contract (APG)
rules.rule: role=dialog + aria-modal=true + an accessible name (aria-labelledby); move focus in on open, TRAP focus within, Esc closes, and RESTORE focus to the trigger on close.
rules.dont: A modal that leaves focus behind it or can't be closed with Esc.

rules.title: Animation from interactions (reduced motion)
rules.rule: Non-essential motion animation triggered by interaction is disabled under @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce).

rules.title: Redundant entry
rules.rule: Information already entered earlier in a multi-step process is auto-populated or selectable — don't make users re-type it.
